operating_state: Total revenue increased 4.0% to $2.77 billion driven by property revenue growth, with U.S. & Canada up on colocation activity and Data Centers up on new lease commencements. Asia-Pacific declined primarily from revenue reserves related to VIL payment shortfalls in India, while Africa grew on fuel cost pass-throughs and tenant additions despite foreign exchange headwinds. Adjusted EBITDA grew 9% to $1.8 billion from improved gross margins across segments.
cost_structure: Property gross margin expanded 5% while services costs declined, with total property costs increasing. Depreciation, amortization and accretion decreased primarily reflecting lower intangible asset amortization and prior-year impairments reducing the depreciable base. Other operating expenses surged including loss on Mexico Fiber sale and increased impairment charges.
material_events: Completed sale of Mexico Fiber assets for $252.5 million generating loss and disposal of goodwill. Issued $1.5 billion in senior notes and completed $1.3 billion securitization transaction through Series 2023-1A Securities backed by mortgages on communication sites. Repaid $1.0 billion of senior notes at maturity and entire Series 2013-2A securities using securitization proceeds.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation provided $232.1 million in other comprehensive income while operational foreign currency losses were recorded compared to gains in prior year. Interest expense increased significantly to $340.2 million from $262.4 million reflecting higher debt levels and rising rates. VIL customer in India continues partial payments creating ongoing collection uncertainty and revenue impact.
forward_outlook: Management expects 2023 capital expenditures of $1.65-$1.76 billion including construction of communication sites globally and data center assets. Maintains significant liquidity through multicurrency and credit facilities with near-term maturities including term loan due December 2023. Exploring strategic alternatives to reduce India exposure including potential equity sales subject to regulatory approvals.
uncertainty_profile: VIL continues partial payments creating ongoing impairment risk for tenant-related intangibles and tower portfolio assets. Securitization agreements require maintaining debt service coverage ratios above 1.30x to avoid cash trapping mechanisms. Unrecognized tax benefits with potential changes expected over the next 12 months from ongoing settlements across multiple foreign jurisdictions.
